skill.name: pennylane
skill.description: Guides users through PennyLane workflows for quantum circuits, quantum machine learning, chemistry simulations, device backends, optimization, and advanced transforms.
skill.summary: Build and optimize PennyLane quantum computing workflows with focused reference guidance.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many severe patterns, but review found the shell execution, weak cryptography, filesystem, reconnaissance, and critical heuristic alerts are false positives from Markdown examples and scientific terminology. No prompt injection or malicious exfiltration intent was found. The remaining concern is that device backend documentation shows API tokens and keys as inline parameters, which can encourage unsafe credential handling.

medium_findings.title: Inline Provider Credential Examples
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The device backend guide shows IBM and IonQ credentials passed directly as code parameters. These are placeholder values, not active secrets, but the pattern can lead users to hardcode real API tokens in notebooks or repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations clearly show token and API key parameters in executable Python examples. Confidence is below very high because the values are placeholders and no secret exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported Ruby backtick execution comes from Markdown fences, inline file references, and installation examples. The skill contains documentation, not an executable Ruby or shell wrapper that runs these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed lines are Markdown code blocks containing package installation commands. There is no code path in the skill that invokes a shell or evaluates user-controlled input.

low_findings.title: Scientific Terms Misclassified as Weak Cryptography or Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak-cryptography and reconnaissance hits occur in PennyLane, quantum chemistry, optimization, and random initialization examples. They do not implement MD5, SHA1, password hashing, host probing, or system inventory collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context is quantum algorithm documentation and best-practice prose. The scanner terms overlap with scientific language, but no cryptographic or reconnaissance behavior appears.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Misclassified as Suspicious Network Activity
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URLs are public PennyLane documentation, tutorial, forum, and GitHub links. They are reference links for users and are not contacted by executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are listed under resource sections and point to expected public project resources. No network client code or credential-bearing request was found.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Findings Are Mathematical List Operations
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The filesystem alerts map to Python list append operations while constructing a QUBO Hamiltonian. They do not read, write, delete, or enumerate local files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines append coefficients and observables to Python lists. There is no filesystem module, path handling, or file operation in the reviewed context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Parameters Shown Inline
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes placeholder token and API key arguments in code examples. Users should replace this pattern with environment variables or secret managers when adapting the examples.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples are explicit and easy to copy into working code. The values are placeholders, so the finding is a secure-use warning rather than evidence of malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Quantum projects require careful setup across circuits, devices, gradients, and chemistry workflows. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused PennyLane references for building and optimizing those workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains PennyLane installation and plugin setup for common quantum backends.
Guides construction of QNodes, gates, measurements, templates, and circuit transforms.
Covers hybrid quantum machine learning with PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow, and PennyLane optimizers.
Provides quantum chemistry workflows for molecular Hamiltonians, VQE, UCCSD, and property calculations.
Summarizes simulator, hardware backend, shot, caching, GPU, and JIT execution options.
Describes optimization strategies for gradients, QAOA, barren plateaus, restarts, and learning schedules.
limitations: It is documentation-only and does not install PennyLane or run circuits by itself.
Hardware access still requires valid provider accounts, plugins, credentials, and available devices.
Examples may need adaptation for current PennyLane plugin versions and backend availability.
It does not validate molecular data, model accuracy, or quantum hardware cost before execution.

use_cases.title: Prototype Quantum Circuits
use_cases.description: Create QNodes, select devices, add measurements, and inspect circuits before moving to larger experiments.

use_cases.title: Train Hybrid Quantum Models
use_cases.description: Design data encodings, variational layers, framework integrations, and optimizers for quantum neural networks.

use_cases.title: Run Chemistry Simulations
use_cases.description: Build molecular Hamiltonians, configure VQE or UCCSD workflows, and compare simulator results for small molecules.

prompt_templates.title: Create a First Circuit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PennyLane skill to help me create a two-qubit QNode on a local simulator. Explain each gate, measurement, and parameter choice.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Backend
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PennyLane skill to compare simulator and hardware backend options for a four-qubit experiment with 1000 shots.

prompt_templates.title: Design a QML Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PennyLane skill to outline a hybrid quantum-classical classifier with data encoding, ansatz selection, optimizer choice, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a VQE Study
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PennyLane skill to plan a VQE workflow for a small molecule, including Hamiltonian generation, ansatz, gradients, convergence checks, and backend tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: I need a PennyLane starting point for a two-qubit optimization problem.
output_examples.output: A concise workflow that selects a local simulator, defines a QNode, adds parameterized rotations, chooses an expectation value, and recommends gradient descent checks.

output_examples.input: I want to move a circuit from simulation to hardware.
output_examples.output: A backend comparison that lists plugin requirements, credential handling cautions, shot configuration, supported operations, and simulator validation steps.

output_examples.input: I am preparing a VQE experiment for a small molecule.
output_examples.output: A structured experiment plan covering molecular geometry, Hamiltonian construction, ansatz choice, optimizer settings, convergence monitoring, and result interpretation.

best_practices: Start on local simulators and validate results before using paid or queued hardware backends.
Store provider credentials outside source files, notebooks, prompts, and shared repositories.
Track gradients, costs, shots, seeds, and device settings so experiments can be reproduced.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real API tokens into copied backend examples or committed notebooks.
Do not switch to hardware before checking circuit depth, operations, shots, and simulator behavior.
Do not assume one optimizer, ansatz, or encoding works across all quantum learning or chemistry tasks.

faq.question: Does this skill run PennyLane code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides reference guidance and examples. Users still run code in their own Python environment.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it help with quantum hardware?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains backend plugins and device setup, but users need their own accounts, credentials, and access permissions.

faq.question: Is it suitable for quantum machine learning?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers data encodings, quantum neural networks, hybrid training, and framework integrations.

faq.question: Does it cover quantum chemistry?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes molecular Hamiltonians, VQE, UCCSD, geometry workflows, and molecular property examples.

faq.question: What is the main security caution?
faq.answer: Some backend examples show inline credential placeholders. Use environment variables or secret managers for real provider credentials.
